stemHtml: Consider a large jet transport aircraft with hydraulically operate brakes and power assisted flight controls. After a complete loss of hydraulic pressure, the pilot was able to manually fly the aircraft to a landing and bring the aircraft to a stop on the runway. How were the brakes able to operate after the hydraulic failure?

options.a: The pilot was able to brake because the accumulator stored enough pressure for the system to keep working.
options.b: The brakes are not required in a hydraulic failure as the aircraft will not be travelling as fast as usual.
options.c: The pilot was able to provide enough pressure from their toe brakes to operate the brakes normally.
options.d: The brakes will automatically lock due to the hydraulic failure leading to maximum deceleration after landing.
